summary: The suite covers all six public functions (parse_package_json, parse_requirements_txt, parse_manifest, load_config, check_compliance, generate_report) with clear per-function test classes and meaningful assertions. Multiple mock strategies (all-approved, mixed, unknown/None) cleanly exercise the three status branches. Good use of pytest's tmp_path keeps tests hermetic. Gaps: no tests for the CLI main() entry point or the built-in mock_license_lookup/MOCK_LICENSE_DB; no end-to-end integration test running a full manifest → config → report pipeline; requirements.txt parser edge cases are underexplored (multi-constraint specs like 'requests>=2,<3', extras notation like 'requests[security]==2.31', ~= and != specifiers, case sensitivity in license names); config with missing allowed_licenses/denied_licenses keys is untested; and the task explicitly required workflow-structure tests (YAML parsing, path existence, actionlint exit code) which are absent. Parameterized tests would reduce duplication across version-specifier variants. Design is clean with well-named classes, inline docstrings, and shared module-level fixtures, though a conftest.py for the common config fixture would be cleaner.
